verses.text: The word comes to Zechariah: 'The LORD hath been sore displeased with your fathers.' A warning recalled. The fate of past generations is set before the present one.
The word comes to Zechariah: 'The LORD hath been sore displeased with your fathers.' A warning recalled. The fate of past generations is set before the present one.
'Turn ye unto me... and I will turn unto you'; be not like your fathers who would not hear. Gracious call. God meets the returning heart with His own returning favour.
'Turn ye unto me... and I will turn unto you'; be not like your fathers who would not hear. Gracious call. God meets the returning heart with His own returning favour.
The fathers are gone, but God's words 'took hold of them,' and they owned His justice. The word endures. Men pass, but God's word is fulfilled to the letter.
The fathers are gone, but God's words 'took hold of them,' and they owned His justice. The word endures. Men pass, but God's word is fulfilled to the letter.
A vision by night: a rider on a red horse among the myrtles, with horses of various colours behind. Watchers abroad. God has His messengers patrolling all the earth.
A vision by night: a rider on a red horse among the myrtles, with horses of various colours behind. Watchers abroad. God has His messengers patrolling all the earth.
A vision by night: a rider on a red horse among the myrtles, with horses of various colours behind. Watchers abroad. God has His messengers patrolling all the earth.
These are sent to walk through the earth, and they report 'all the earth sitteth still, and is at rest.' False calm. The nations are at ease while God's people still mourn.
These are sent to walk through the earth, and they report 'all the earth sitteth still, and is at rest.' False calm. The nations are at ease while God's people still mourn.
The angel pleads, 'O LORD... how long wilt thou not have mercy on Jerusalem?' and God answers with 'good... and comfortable words.' Heaven intercedes. God's reply to His grieving people is kindness.
The angel pleads, 'O LORD... how long wilt thou not have mercy on Jerusalem?' and God answers with 'good... and comfortable words.' Heaven intercedes. God's reply to His grieving people is kindness.
He is jealous for Jerusalem, displeased with the heathen at ease; His house shall be built, and His cities spread with good. Mercy returns. God will restore and prosper the city He loves.
He is jealous for Jerusalem, displeased with the heathen at ease; His house shall be built, and His cities spread with good. Mercy returns. God will restore and prosper the city He loves.
He is jealous for Jerusalem, displeased with the heathen at ease; His house shall be built, and His cities spread with good. Mercy returns. God will restore and prosper the city He loves.
He is jealous for Jerusalem, displeased with the heathen at ease; His house shall be built, and His cities spread with good. Mercy returns. God will restore and prosper the city He loves.
He sees four horns that scattered Judah, and four smiths come to cast them down. Powers overthrown. God raises means to break whatever broke His people.
